questions.question: Explain the copy constructor in C++ with its syntax and when it is invoked.
keyPoints: Copy constructor is a special constructor that initializes a new object from an existing object of the same class
Syntax: ClassName(const ClassName& obj)
Called in three scenarios: (1) When object passed by value to function (2) When object returned by value (3) When object is initialized from another object

questions.question: What is the difference between shallow copy and deep copy? When is deep copy necessary?
keyPoints: Shallow copy copies pointer addresses - both objects share same memory
Deep copy allocates new memory and copies actual data content
Deep copy necessary when class contains pointer members to dynamic memory to avoid double-free and memory issues

questions.question: Explain the Rule of Three in C++. Why is it important?
keyPoints: Rule of Three states if a class defines any one of: destructor, copy constructor, or copy assignment operator, it should define all three
Important because these three functions are interdependent for proper resource management
Without all three, shallow copy by default can cause resource leaks or double-free errors

questions.question: What is the difference between copy constructor and copy assignment operator?
keyPoints: Copy constructor is called when new object is being created and initialized
Copy assignment operator is called when assigning to an already existing object
Different invocation contexts: initialization vs assignment to existing object

questions.question: How can you prevent copying of objects in a class? Explain with example.
keyPoints: Method 1: Declare copy constructor as private
Method 2: Use C++11 '= delete' syntax: ClassName(const ClassName&) = delete;
Both methods cause compilation error when copying is attempted

questions.question: Write a C++ class with a copy constructor that performs deep copy for a string member.
keyPoints: Class with char* or std::string member
User-defined copy constructor allocating new memory
Copy actual string content to new memory
Properly manage memory to avoid leaks

questions.question: Explain why const and reference (&) are used in copy constructor parameter declaration.
keyPoints: const prevents modification of source object during copy operation
Reference (&) prevents infinite recursion (pass-by-value would call copy constructor)
Reference allows passing without creating a copy of the argument itself

questions.question: Define a class 'String' with char* data member. Implement (a) Constructor (b) Copy Constructor (c) Copy Assignment Operator (d) Destructor. Explain the need for deep copy implementation.
keyPoints: Define String class with char* member storing dynamic memory
Constructor allocates memory and copies string
Copy constructor allocates new memory and copies content (deep copy)
Copy assignment checks for self-assignment, deallocates old memory, allocates new and copies
Destructor deallocates dynamic memory
Need for deep copy: default shallow copy causes double-free when both objects destroyed

questions.question: Explain the Rule of Five in C++11. Write a class demonstrating all five special member functions.
keyPoints: Rule of Five: Destructor, Copy Constructor, Copy Assignment, Move Constructor, Move Assignment
All five needed for proper resource management in modern C++
Move semantics introduced in C++11 for efficient resource transfer
Demonstrate class with all five functions properly implemented
Explain when each is called and their roles

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program demonstrating copy constructor usage with a class containing dynamic memory. Show all scenarios where copy constructor is called.
keyPoints: Create class with dynamic memory (e.g., Array class)
Demonstrate: (1) Initialization: Class obj2 = obj1 (2) Pass by value to function (3) Return by value from function
Show proper deep copy implementation
Display output showing copy constructor invocation in each case
Handle memory properly to avoid leaks

questions.question: What is copy elision? Explain Return Value Optimization (RVO) with an example. How does it affect copy constructor calls?
keyPoints: Copy elision is compiler optimization that removes unnecessary copy/move operations
RVO: compiler creates object directly in caller's memory instead of returning temporary
NRVO: similar optimization for named variables
Can make copy constructor calls implicit and invisible in code
Even with elision, copy constructor must still be correctly defined
